task_completion.description: Open the vulnerable PL/SQL package source code.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor, IDE, or database client displaying the source code for the 'HR_LEGACY_REPORTING' package, potentially opened from '/home/ga/Desktop/vulnerable_source.sql'.

task_completion.description: Refactor SEARCH_EMPLOYEES to use bind variables.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the modified code show the 'SEARCH_EMPLOYEES' procedure using a bind variable in its dynamic SQL. Look for 'EXECUTE IMMEDIATE ... USING <variable>' instead of string concatenation (||) for the name pattern filter.

task_completion.description: Refactor RANK_DEPARTMENTS to use DBMS_ASSERT or a whitelist.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the modified code show the 'RANK_DEPARTMENTS' procedure validating the sort column. Look for the use of 'DBMS_ASSERT' (e.g., DBMS_ASSERT.SIMPLE_SQL_NAME) or a logical whitelist (e.g., IF/CASE statements checking the column name) before the EXECUTE IMMEDIATE statement.

task_completion.description: Compile the secured package in the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a terminal (e.g., using sqlplus) or a database GUI connected as the 'hr' user executing the 'CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE BODY HR_LEGACY_REPORTING' statement, followed by a success message such as 'Package body created' or 'compiled'.

task_completion.description: Export the fixed package body code to the required desktop file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the secured PL/SQL code being saved to '/home/ga/Desktop/secure_reporting.sql'. This can be seen in a text editor's 'Save As' dialog, a terminal command (like cp or redirecting output to the file), or the file appearing on the Desktop.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually modified the PL/SQL code rather than just creating an empty or dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file '/home/ga/Desktop/secure_reporting.sql' should contain actual PL/SQL code with the CREATE OR REPLACE PACKAGE BODY statement, not just placeholder text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent interacted with the Oracle database to compile the code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for a database client interface (like sqlplus, SQL Developer, or DBeaver) showing an active connection to localhost:1521/XEPDB1 and the execution of SQL commands, rather than just editing text files.

integrity.description: Verify the agent manually applied the fixes or used a script to do so, rather than pasting a pre-computed perfect solution without inspecting the original code.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent reading the original vulnerable code or querying the database for the package source before writing the fixes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Package Name: HR_LEGACY_REPORTING. Vulnerable Source: /home/ga/Desktop/vulnerable_source.sql. Output File: /home/ga/Desktop/secure_reporting.sql. To verify the fixes: SEARCH_EMPLOYEES must use 'USING' for bind variables in EXECUTE IMMEDIATE. RANK_DEPARTMENTS must wrap the sort column variable in a DBMS_ASSERT function (like SIMPLE_SQL_NAME or ENQUOTE_NAME) or use an explicit IF/ELSIF/CASE whitelist.
